title: Racosperma pritzelianum
taxonomy: Racosperma pritzelianum is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a type of flowering plant that is native to Western Australia. The species was previously classified as Acacia pritzelianum but has since been reclassified under the genus Racosperma. The classification change reflects the ongoing reevaluation of the Acacia genus and its relationship to other plant families. Racosperma pritzelianum is a small shrub that is found in arid regions.

intro: Racosperma pritzelianum, also known as Pritzel's wattle, is a plant species native to Western Australia, characterized by its woody shrub habit and yellow flowers. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Racosperma pritzelianum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
